zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Jane Buckingham於1470年1月1日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾文時，她的父親John Stafford of Buckingham25歲，母親Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham25歲。她於1490年與Sir Thomas Wheeler在英國貝德福爾可藍費爾德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1540年12月19日在英國貝德福爾可藍費爾德享年70歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾可藍費爾德聖 彼得 安德 聖 包利斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Jane Buckingham于1470年1月1日出生在英国巴金哈姆爾文时，她的父亲John Stafford of Buckingham25岁，母亲Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham25岁。她于1490年与Sir Thomas Wheeler在英国貝德福爾可藍費爾德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1540年12月19日在英国貝德福爾可藍費爾德享年70岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾可藍費爾德聖 彼得 安德 聖 包利斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Buckingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the former county seat of the county of Buckinghamshire, Old English Buccingahamm ‘water meadow (Old English hamm) of the people of (-inga-) Bucc(a)’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Jane Buckingham was born on 1 January 1470, in Wing, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, John Stafford of Buckingham, was 25 and her mother, Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham, was 25. She married Sir Thomas Wheeler in 1490, in Cranfield, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 19 December 1540, in Cranfield, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in St Peter and St Paul's Church, Cranfield, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Jane Buckingham est née le 1 janvier 1470, à Wing, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Stafford of Buckingham, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Wheeler en 1490, à Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 décembre 1540, à Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à St Peter and St Paul's Church, Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Jane Buckinghamが1470年1月1日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウィングで生まれた時，父John Stafford of Buckinghamは25歳，母Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckinghamは25歳であった。1490年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアクランフィールドでSir Thomas Wheelerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1540年12月19日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアクランフィールドで70歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアクランフィールドスト ペター アンド スト プアウル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter and St Paul's Church, Cranfield: 영국 베드퍼드실르 크랜필드 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Joan Jane Buckingham(이)가 1470년 1월 1일 영국 벅잉함실르 윙에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Stafford of Buckingham(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 베드퍼드실르 크랜필드에서 Sir Thomas Wheeler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1540년 12월 19일 영국 베드퍼드실르 크랜필드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 크랜필드 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Jane Buckingham nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1470, em Wing, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Stafford of Buckingham, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Wheeler em 1490, em Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 19 de dezembro de 1540, em Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em St Peter and St Paul's Church, Cranfield, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.St Peter and St Paul's Church, Cranfield: ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, кранфиелд, бедфордшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Jane Buckingham родилась 1 января 1470 г. в местности виг, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Stafford of Buckingham было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Advice of Upton Countess of Buckingham, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Wheeler в 1490 г. в местности кранфиелд, бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 19 декабря 1540 г. в местности кранфиелд, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, кранфиелд, бедфордшир, Англия.
